writer pod
libmxl.FlowWriter
mxlCreateFlowWriter →
OpenGrain(i) → fill payload → Commit

div.div:
source gateway pod
(hostNetwork)
libmxl.FlowReader
fabrics.RegionsForFlowReader
fabrics.Initiator → AddTarget
transfer goroutine: TransferGrain

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 760px; height: 1px; padding-top: 563px; margin-left: 40px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Per-grain commit on the source side
1. Writer's libmxl fills the payload directly in its mapping of g[i] - bytes hit tmpfs pages
2. Commit advances HeadIndex (one counter in the shared mapping)
3. Source gateway's transfer goroutine polls Reader.Runtime() and sees the new HeadIndex -
the gateway maps the same RAM, so no copy or signal is needed for it to see the change
4. Initiator.TransferGrain reads from the registered region and issues a libfabric RDMA write

switch.text: Per-grain commit on the source side...

div.div:
target gateway pod
(hostNetwork)
libmxl.FlowWriter
fabrics.RegionsForFlowWriter
fabrics.Target (publishes targetInfo)
progress loop: ReadGrainNonBlocking

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 280px; height: 1px; padding-top: 387px; margin-left: 1362px;

div.div:
consumer pod
libmxl.FlowReader
LD_PRELOAD libmxl-intent.so
(applies only on openat; reads are
direct mmap, no shim involvement)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 760px; height: 1px; padding-top: 563px; margin-left: 890px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Per-grain receive on the target side
1. Source gateway's libfabric initiator RDMA-writes payload + grain header straight into this
node's registered region - the bytes land directly in tmpfs pages, no kernel copy
2. Target gateway's Target.ReadGrainNonBlocking returns idx=i
3. Writer.OpenGrain(i).Commit advances HeadIndex (metadata-only - payload is already in place)
4. Consumer's FlowReader sees HeadIndex and GetGrain returns a pointer into its mapping of g[i]

switch.text: Per-grain receive on the target side...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 335px; margin-left: 1168px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: center;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); white-space: nowrap;

display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); white-space: nowrap;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1670px; height: 1px; padding-top: 703px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
End-to-end bytes path:
writer userspace → tmpfs page → libfabric NIC → (wire) → target NIC → tmpfs page → consumer userspace.
No process holds an intermediate copy. libmxl's coordination is HeadIndex updates in shared memory; libfabric's transport is one-sided RDMA into a registered region.
A real node usually plays exactly one role per flow - it is either the source's tmpfs+gateway, or a target's tmpfs+gateway+consumer.

switch.text: End-to-end bytes path: writer userspace → tmpfs page → libfabric NIC → (wire) → target NIC → tmpfs page → consumer userspace....
